title: How to make a LUT file

messages.content: # Want to make a LUT file for your Logitech? Follow this Guide!

# How to use:
## Step 1
> Run WheelCheck

## Step 2 
> "Max Count" should be at least 50 in box. Lower values work, but 50 or even 100 give better results.

## Step 3
> Select "Step Log 2 (linear force test)" and wait until the test stops.
> The wheel will start to rotate back and forth, **DO NOT** touch it as it will make your LUT file heavily inaccurate.

## Step 4
> Extract LUTGenerator.zip that you have just downloaded and run LUTGenerator.exe

## Step 5
> Open the csv that was just generated by WheelCheck.

## Step 6
> Save the generated LUT in My Documents\Assetto Corsa\cfg\ folder, eg ***myLUT.lut***

## Step 7
> Open ff_post_process.ini located in My Documents\Assetto Corsa\cfg\ and make sure the TYPE=LUT, ENABLED=1 and CURVE = Generated LUT filename

## **After this, do not alter the FFB Strength in your wheel's profiler!** If you change the FFB Gain in your wheel's profiler, you should rerun wheelcheck+lut generator.

> For example, if the file you produced with LUTGenerator is myLUT.lut, then this is how the ff_post_process.ini should look:
────────────────
> [HEADER]
> VERSION=1
> TYPE=**LUT**
> ENABLED=**1**

> [GAMMA]
> VALUE=1

> [LUT]
> CURVE=**myLUT.lut**
────────────────
> **It is recommended to do a LUT file once every month.**
